card.intuition: A collaborative process where team members examine each other's code to catch bugs, improve quality, and share knowledge.
card.analogy: A proofreader for a book: even the best author makes typos. A second pair of eyes finds things the author was 'too close' to see, and they help the author write in a way that the whole team can understand.
when_to_use: Every time a Pull Request is opened
Onboarding new team members to learn the style guide
Mentoring junior developers by providing constructive feedback
gotchas: Nit-picking: don't argue about semicolons or tabs. Use an automated Linter and Formatter for that! Reviews should focus on logic, security, and design.
Review Fatigue: if a Pull Request has 1,000 changes, no one will read it properly. Keep PRs small (< 400 lines).

exercise.prompt: What is the recommended maximum number of lines of code (LOC) for a single pull request to ensure high-quality review?
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return int(ans) <= 500
assert check("400")
print("PASS")
